axiom obj : Type
axiom person : obj
axiom SavesAggressively : obj → Prop
axiom ImprovesCredit : obj → Prop
axiom AffordsDownPayment : obj → Prop
axiom QualifiesMortgage : obj → Prop
axiom BuysDreamHome : obj → Prop

axiom Save_implies_Afford : ∀ x : obj, SavesAggressively x → AffordsDownPayment x
axiom Improve_implies_Qualify : ∀ x : obj, ImprovesCredit x → QualifiesMortgage x
axiom Qualify_implies_Buy : ∀ x : obj, QualifiesMortgage x → BuysDreamHome x

axiom Save_or_Improve : SavesAggressively person ∨ ImprovesCredit person
axiom Not_Afford : ¬ AffordsDownPayment person

theorem buy_dream_home : BuysDreamHome person := by
  -- derive ¬SavesAggressively person from ¬AffordsDownPayment and (Saves -> Affords)
  have notS : ¬ SavesAggressively person := by
    intro hS
    have hA : AffordsDownPayment person := Save_implies_Afford person hS
    exact Not_Afford hA
  -- from S ∨ C and ¬S, get ImprovesCredit person
  have hC : ImprovesCredit person := by
    cases Save_or_Improve with
    | inl hS =>
      have : False := notS hS
      exact False.elim this
    | inr hI => exact hI
  -- then qualify and buy
  have hQ : QualifiesMortgage person := Improve_implies_Qualify person hC
  exact Qualify_implies_Buy person hQ
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

axiom obj : Type
axiom Amy : obj

axiom PracticeDaily : obj → Prop
axiom MuscleMemory : obj → Prop
axiom PlaySimpleSongs : obj → Prop
axiom PlayAnySongs : obj → Prop
axiom LearnMusicTheory : obj → Prop
axiom KnowSongStructure : obj → Prop
axiom AnalyzeComposition : obj → Prop

-- If someone practiced basic chords daily, they develop muscle memory.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, PracticeDaily x → MuscleMemory x
-- If someone developed muscle memory, they can play simple songs.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, MuscleMemory x → PlaySimpleSongs x
-- If someone learned music theory, they gained knowledge about song structure.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, LearnMusicTheory x → KnowSongStructure x
-- Knowledge about song structure enables analysis/understanding of composition.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, KnowSongStructure x → AnalyzeComposition x
-- Either (for Amy) she was practicing daily or focused on learning music theory (or both).
axiom A5 : PracticeDaily Amy ∨ LearnMusicTheory Amy
-- Being able to play simple songs implies being able to play some songs.
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, PlaySimpleSongs x → PlayAnySongs x

theorem amy_knows_if_cannot_play : (¬ PlayAnySongs Amy) → KnowSongStructure Amy := by
  intro hCannotAny
  -- From not being able to play any songs, infer not able to play simple songs.
  have hNotSimple : ¬ PlaySimpleSongs Amy := by
    intro hSimple
    have hAny : PlayAnySongs Amy := A6 Amy hSimple
    exact hCannotAny hAny
  -- Case analysis on whether Amy practiced daily or learned theory.
  have hdisj := A5
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hPractice =>
    -- Practicing leads to simple songs, contradicting not-simple; derive knowledge by explosion.
    have hMM : MuscleMemory Amy := A1 Amy hPractice
    have hSimple : PlaySimpleSongs Amy := A2 Amy hMM
    have hFalse : False := hNotSimple hSimple
    exact False.elim hFalse
  | inr hTheory =>
    -- If she learned theory, she has knowledge about song structure.
    have hKnow : KnowSongStructure Amy := A3 Amy hTheory
    exact hKnow
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

theorem not_todolist_of_not_relax : ¬ Relaxes Alex → ¬ ToDoList Alex := by
  intro hNotRelax
  -- From Weekend → Relaxes Alex and ¬Relaxes Alex, derive ¬Weekend
  have hNotWeekend : ¬ Weekend := by
    intro hW
    have hR : Relaxes Alex := P1 Alex hW
    exact hNotRelax hR
  -- From Weekend ∨ ¬ClearMind Alex and ¬Weekend, derive ¬ClearMind Alex
  have hOr := P3 Alex
  have hNotCM : ¬ ClearMind Alex := by
    cases hOr with
    | inl hW => exact hNotWeekend hW
    | inr hN => exact hN
  -- Show ¬ToDoList Alex by chaining ToDoList → Organized → Finishes → ClearMind and contradicting ¬ClearMind
  intro hTodo
  have hOrg : Organized Alex := P5 Alex hTodo
  have hFin : Finishes Alex := P4 Alex hOrg
  have hCM  : ClearMind Alex := P2 Alex hFin
  exact hNotCM hCM
</lean>

axiom obj : Type
axiom Student : obj

axiom Study : obj → Prop
axiom Participate : obj → Prop
axiom Excel : obj → Prop
axiom Leader : obj → Prop
axiom Prefect : obj → Prop

-- If the student studies, they excel.
axiom study_imp_excel : Study Student → Excel Student
-- If the student participates, they gain leadership.
axiom participate_imp_leader : Participate Student → Leader Student
-- The student either studies or participates.
axiom study_or_participate : Study Student ∨ Participate Student
-- If someone is a leader, they are selected as class prefect (general).
axiom leader_imp_prefect : ∀ x : obj, Leader x → Prefect x
-- Given: the student did not excel.
axiom not_excel : ¬ Excel Student

theorem student_chosen_prefect : Prefect Student := by
  -- From not excel and study → excel, derive not study.
  have h_notStudy : ¬ Study Student := by
    intro hs
    apply not_excel
    apply study_imp_excel
    exact hs
  -- Using the disjunction and not study, deduce participate.
  have h_part : Participate Student := by
    cases study_or_participate with
    | inl hs =>
        exact False.elim (h_notStudy hs)
    | inr hp =>
        exact hp
  -- From participate, get leader.
  have h_leader : Leader Student := by
    apply participate_imp_leader
    exact h_part
  -- From leader, get prefect.
  have h_pref : Leader Student → Prefect Student := leader_imp_prefect Student
  apply h_pref
  exact h_leader
</lean>

-- 2) If someone feels a chill, they turn up the heat.

      exact hS
  -- Chain implications to Flex
  have hRec : Recovery Lauren := A3 Lauren hStretch
  exact A4 Lauren hRec

axiom RunningImproved : obj → Prop
axiom Flexible : obj → Prop
axiom LessProneToInjury : obj → Prop
axiom GoalTimeAchieved : obj → Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, SprintingFrequently x → RunningImproved x
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, RunningImproved x → GoalTimeAchieved x
axiom P3 : SprintingFrequently Jesse ∨ StretchingClasses Jesse
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, StretchingClasses x → Flexible x ∧ LessProneToInjury x

-- Given statement to evaluate (assumed as fact)
axiom NotImproved_Jesse : ¬ RunningImproved Jesse

theorem jesse_flexible_and_less_prone : Flexible Jesse ∧ LessProneToInjury Jesse := by
  -- From not improved and P1, Jesse was not sprinting frequently
  have not_sprint : ¬ SprintingFrequently Jesse := by
    intro hs
    have imp : RunningImproved Jesse := P1 Jesse hs
    exact NotImproved_Jesse imp
  -- From the disjunction P3 and not_sprint, conclude stretching classes
  have stretch : StretchingClasses Jesse := by
    cases P3 with
    | inl hs => exact False.elim (not_sprint hs)
    | inr ht => exact ht
  -- Apply P4 to get flexible and less prone to injury
  exact P4 Jesse stretch

<lean>
axiom Person : Type
axiom Max : Person

axiom PackageOnTime : Prop
axiom SpecialReady : Prop

axiom Starts : Person → Prop
axiom Learns : Person → Prop
axiom Pickup : Person → Prop

-- If the package arrives on time, people will start a new project.
axiom R1 : PackageOnTime → ∀ x : Person, Starts x
-- If the special order is ready, they can pick up the materials they need (treated as: they pick up).
axiom R2 : SpecialReady → ∀ x : Person, Pickup x
-- Either the package will arrive on time or the special order will be ready.
axiom R3 : PackageOnTime ∨ SpecialReady
-- If someone starts his new project, they will learn some new skills.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : Person, Starts x → Learns x

-- Max was unable to pick up the materials he needed.
axiom MaxUnable : ¬ Pickup Max

theorem max_learned_skills : Learns Max := by
  -- From Max's inability to pick up materials and R2, conclude the special order is not ready.
  have notReady : ¬ SpecialReady := by
    intro hReady
    have pickMax : Pickup Max := (R2 hReady) Max
    exact MaxUnable pickMax
  -- From the disjunction and notReady, conclude the package is on time.
  have onTime : PackageOnTime :=
    Or.elim R3 (fun h => h) (fun h => False.elim (notReady h))
  -- Then Max starts a new project.
  have startMax : Starts Max := (R1 onTime) Max
  -- Hence Max learns some new skills.
  exact R4 Max startMax
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

<lean>
axiom Person : Type
axiom Jane : Person

axiom Exercises : Person → Prop
axiom EatsNutritious : Person → Prop
axiom Fit : Person → Prop
axiom Energetic : Person → Prop
axiom ValuesWellness : Person → Prop

-- If people exercise regularly, they are fit.
axiom R_ex_fit : ∀ x : Person, Exercises x → Fit x
-- If people eat nutritious foods, they have energy.
axiom R_eat_energy : ∀ x : Person, EatsNutritious x → Energetic x
-- Not everyone in town was active or energetic (model "active" as "fit").
axiom Not_all_fit_or_energetic : ¬ ∀ x : Person, Fit x ∨ Energetic x
-- If someone values wellness, they exercise regularly.
axiom R_val_ex : ∀ x : Person, ValuesWellness x → Exercises x

-- Given: Jane eats very nutritious foods.
axiom Jane_eats : EatsNutritious Jane

-- From the context, the mayor's conclusion can be derived.
theorem mayor_conclusion : ∃ x : Person, ¬ Exercises x ∨ ¬ EatsNutritious x := by
  classical
  have hx : ∃ x : Person, ¬ (Fit x ∨ Energetic x) := by
    by_contra hcontra
    have hall : ∀ x : Person, Fit x ∨ Energetic x := by
      intro x
      by_contra hx'
      apply hcontra
      exact Exists.intro x hx'
    exact Not_all_fit_or_energetic hall
  rcases hx with ⟨w, hw⟩
  have hnf : ¬ Fit w := by
    intro hf
    exact hw (Or.inl hf)
  have hne : ¬ Energetic w := by
    intro he
    exact hw (Or.inr he)
  have hnotEx : ¬ Exercises w := by
    intro ex
    have : Fit w := R_ex_fit w ex
    exact hnf this
  have hnotEat : ¬ EatsNutritious w := by
    intro eat
    have : Energetic w := R_eat_energy w eat
    exact hne this
  exact Exists.intro w (Or.inl hnotEx)

-- What we can derive about Jane from her eating nutritious foods:
theorem jane_energetic : Energetic Jane := by
  have h := R_eat_energy Jane
  exact h Jane_eats
</lean>

    exact ⟨w, Or.inl hnotEx⟩

-- What we can derive about Jane from her eating nutritious foods:

  R_eat_energy Jane Jane_eats

axiom PricesUp : Prop

axiom P2 : MakeList Mary → Plan Mary
axiom P3 : Sale ∨ (¬ Plan Mary) ∨ (Sale ∧ ¬ Plan Mary)
axiom P4 : Payday → MakeList Mary
axiom P5 : Payday ∨ PricesUp

theorem mary_prices_up : (¬ StockUp Mary) → PricesUp := by
  intro hNotStock
  -- Analyze the trilemma about sale/planning
  cases P3 with
  | inl hSale =>
      have hSU : StockUp Mary := P1 hSale
      exact False.elim (hNotStock hSU)
  | inr hRest =>
      cases hRest with
      | inl hNotPlan =>
          -- From Payday → MakeList and MakeList → Plan, get Payday → Plan
          -- Then with ¬Plan, we get ¬Payday
          have hNotPayday : ¬ Payday := by
            intro hPay
            have hList : MakeList Mary := P4 hPay
            have hPlan : Plan Mary := P2 hList
            exact hNotPlan hPlan
          -- From Payday ∨ PricesUp and ¬Payday, deduce PricesUp
          cases P5 with
          | inl hPay => exact False.elim (hNotPayday hPay)
          | inr hUp  => exact hUp
      | inr hSaleAndNotPlan =>
          have hSale : Sale := hSaleAndNotPlan.left
          have hSU : StockUp Mary := P1 hSale
          exact False.elim (hNotStock hSU)
</lean>

axiom GoodReviews : Prop
axiom ManyPeople : Prop
axiom LowAttendance : Prop
axiom BigStar : Prop
axiom Sequel : Prop

-- If the movie gets good reviews, then a lot of people will go see it.
axiom good_implies_many : GoodReviews → ManyPeople
-- If a big star is in the movie, then it got good reviews.
axiom star_implies_good : BigStar → GoodReviews
-- Either there is a big star in it, or it is a sequel.
axiom star_or_sequel : BigStar ∨ Sequel
-- If attendance is low, then not many people went to see it.
axiom low_implies_not_many : LowAttendance → ¬ ManyPeople

theorem low_attendance_implies_sequel : LowAttendance → Sequel := by
  intro hLow
  cases star_or_sequel with
  | inl hStar =>
    have hGood : GoodReviews := star_implies_good hStar
    have hMany : ManyPeople := good_implies_many hGood
    have hNotMany : ¬ ManyPeople := low_implies_not_many hLow
    have hFalse : False := hNotMany hMany
    exact False.elim hFalse
  | inr hSeq =>
    exact hSeq
</lean>

axiom Rain : Prop
axiom BringUmbrella : obj → Prop
axiom BigGame : Prop
axiom Gather : Prop
axiom HighView : Prop
axiom GoPlay : Prop

-- If rain is forecast, people will bring an umbrella (simplified, ignoring the "when they go out" guard)
axiom Rain_implies_BringUmbrella : Rain → ∀ x : obj, BringUmbrella x
-- If there is a big game on TV, friends get together to watch it
axiom BigGame_implies_Gather : BigGame → Gather
-- Either rain is forecasted, or friends did not get together to watch
axiom Rain_or_notGather : Rain ∨ ¬Gather
-- If the TV viewership is high, then there is a big game on TV
axiom HighView_implies_BigGame : HighView → BigGame
-- Either the TV viewership is high, or they all decide to go play out
axiom HighView_or_GoPlay : HighView ∨ GoPlay

theorem james_noUmbrella_entails_goPlay : ¬BringUmbrella James → GoPlay := by
  intro hNoUmb
  -- From ¬BringUmbrella James, deduce ¬Rain
  have hNotRain : ¬Rain := by
    intro hR
    have hUmb : BringUmbrella James := (Rain_implies_BringUmbrella hR) James
    exact hNoUmb hUmb
  -- From Rain ∨ ¬Gather and ¬Rain, deduce ¬Gather
  have hNotGather : ¬Gather := Or.elim Rain_or_notGather
    (fun hR => False.elim (hNotRain hR))
    (fun hng => hng)
  -- From HighView → BigGame → Gather and ¬Gather, deduce ¬HighView
  have hNotHighView : ¬HighView := by
    intro hHV
    have hBG : BigGame := HighView_implies_BigGame hHV
    have hG : Gather := BigGame_implies_Gather hBG
    exact hNotGather hG
  -- From HighView ∨ GoPlay and ¬HighView, conclude GoPlay
  exact Or.elim HighView_or_GoPlay
    (fun hHV => False.elim (hNotHighView hHV))
    (fun hGP => hGP)
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

axiom obj : Type
axiom John : obj

axiom Sale : Prop
axiom BeginMonth : Prop
axiom BuyMore : obj → Prop
axiom Shop : obj → Prop
axiom GotAll : obj → Prop
axiom FavSoldOut : obj → Prop

-- If the store has a sale, then people will buy more items.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, Sale → BuyMore x
-- If someone goes grocery shopping, then they will get everything on their list.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, Shop x → GotAll x
-- Either the store has a sale, or they did not get everything on their list, or there was a sale and they still did not get all their groceries.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, Sale ∨ ¬ GotAll x ∨ (Sale ∧ ¬ GotAll x)
-- If it is the beginning of the month, then people go grocery shopping.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, BeginMonth → Shop x
-- Either it is the beginning of the month, or their favorite cereal is sold out, or maybe both are true.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, BeginMonth ∨ FavSoldOut x ∨ (BeginMonth ∧ FavSoldOut x)

theorem john_favorite_cereal_sold_out (hNB : ¬ BuyMore John) : FavSoldOut John := by
  -- From "not buy more", deduce no sale
  have hNotSale : ¬ Sale := by
    intro hSale
    have : BuyMore John := R1 John hSale
    exact hNB this
  -- Use the disjunction to get ¬GotAll John
  have hNotGotAll : ¬ GotAll John := by
    have h := R3 John
    cases h with
    | inl hSale =>
        exact False.elim (hNotSale hSale)
    | inr h' =>
        cases h' with
        | inl hNotAll =>
            exact hNotAll
        | inr hBoth =>
            exact hBoth.right
  -- From ¬GotAll and "Shop → GotAll", deduce ¬Shop
  have hNotShop : ¬ Shop John := by
    intro hShop
    have : GotAll John := R2 John hShop
    exact hNotGotAll this
  -- From ¬Shop and "BeginMonth → Shop", deduce ¬BeginMonth
  have hNotBegin : ¬ BeginMonth := by
    intro hBM
    have : Shop John := R4 John hBM
    exact hNotShop this
  -- From the month-or-soldout disjunction, conclude FavSoldOut John
  have hFav : FavSoldOut John := by
    have h := R5 John
    cases h with
    | inl hBM =>
        exact False.elim (hNotBegin hBM)
    | inr h' =>
        cases h' with
        | inl hSold =>
            exact hSold
        | inr hBoth =>
            exact hBoth.right
  exact hFav
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

axiom obj : Type

-- Predicates on people (actions)
axiom AddsSalt : obj → Prop
axiom Stirs : obj → Prop
axiom Tastes : obj → Prop
axiom PreparesSalad : obj → Prop

-- Global stew properties
axiom Savory : Prop
axiom Thickens : Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, AddsSalt x → Savory
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Stirs x → Thickens
axiom Law1 : (∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) ∨ (¬ Thickens ∨ ((∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) ∧ ¬ Thickens))
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Tastes x → Stirs x
axiom Law2 : (∃ x : obj, Tastes x) ∨ ((∃ x : obj, PreparesSalad x) ∨ ((∃ x : obj, Tastes x) ∧ (∃ x : obj, PreparesSalad x)))

theorem salad_prepared_of_not_savory (hns : ¬ Savory) : ∃ x : obj, PreparesSalad x := by
  -- From "if someone adds salt then savory" and "not savory", deduce no one adds salt
  have hexSalt_imp_savory : (∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) → Savory := by
    intro hex
    cases hex with
    | intro w hw => exact P1 w hw
  have noSalt : ¬ (∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) := by
    intro hex
    exact hns (hexSalt_imp_savory hex)

  -- Use Law1 to deduce the stew does not thicken
  have notThick : ¬ Thickens := by
    cases Law1 with
    | inl hSalt =>
      have : False := noSalt hSalt
      exact False.elim this
    | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hnt => exact hnt
      | inr hpair => exact hpair.right

  -- From tasting implies stirring implies thickening, deduce no one tastes
  have hexTaste_imp_thick : (∃ x : obj, Tastes x) → Thickens := by
    intro hex
    cases hex with
    | intro w hw =>
      have hs := P3 w hw
      exact P2 w hs
  have noTaste : ¬ (∃ x : obj, Tastes x) := by
    intro hex
    exact notThick (hexTaste_imp_thick hex)

  -- Use Law2 and the fact that no one tastes to conclude someone prepares a salad
  cases Law2 with
  | inl hTaste =>
    exact False.elim (noTaste hTaste)
  | inr h =>
    cases h with
    | inl hSalad => exact hSalad
    | inr hpair => exact hpair.right
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Stirs x → Thickens
axiom Law1 : (∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) ∨ (¬ Thickens ∨ ((∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) ∧ ¬ Thickens))
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Tastes x → Stirs x
axiom Law2 : (∃ x : obj, Tastes x) ∨ ((∃ x : obj, PreparesSalad x) ∨ ((∃ x : obj, Tastes x) ∧ (∃ x : obj, PreparesSalad x)))

theorem salad_prepared_of_not_savory (hns : ¬ Savory) : ∃ x : obj, PreparesSalad x := by
  -- From "if someone adds salt then savory" and "not savory", deduce no one adds salt
  have hexSalt_imp_savory : (∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) → Savory := by
    intro hex
    cases hex with
    | intro w hw => exact P1 w hw
  have noSalt : ¬ (∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) := by
    intro hex
    exact hns (hexSalt_imp_savory hex)

  -- Use Law1 to deduce the stew does not thicken
  have notThick : ¬ Thickens := by
    cases Law1 with
    | inl hSalt =>
      have : False := noSalt hSalt
      exact False.elim this
    | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hnt => exact hnt
      | inr hpair => exact hpair.right

  -- From tasting implies stirring implies thickening, deduce no one tastes
  have hexTaste_imp_thick : (∃ x : obj, Tastes x) → Thickens := by
    intro hex
    cases hex with
    | intro w hw =>
      have hs := P3 w hw
      exact P2 w hs
  have noTaste : ¬ (∃ x : obj, Tastes x) := by
    intro hex
    exact notThick (hexTaste_imp_thick hex)

  -- Use Law2 and the fact that no one tastes to conclude someone prepares a salad
  cases Law2 with
  | inl hTaste =>
    exact False.elim (noTaste hTaste)
  | inr h =>
    cases h with
    | inl hSalad => exact hSalad
    | inr hpair => exact hpair.right

axiom TimeToGetReady : Prop

axiom WentToBedLate : obj → Prop

-- If the alarm went off, then it was time to get ready.
axiom R_alarm_time : Alarm → TimeToGetReady
-- If they missed the bus, then they'd be late for school.
axiom R_missbus_late : ∀ x : obj, MissBus x → Late x
-- Either the alarm went off, or they weren't late for school.
axiom R_alarm_or_notlate : ∀ x : obj, Alarm ∨ ¬ Late x
-- If they got up late, then they miss the bus.
axiom R_uplate_missbus : ∀ x : obj, GotUpLate x → MissBus x
-- If they went to bed late, then they get up late.
axiom R_bedlate_uplate : ∀ x : obj, WentToBedLate x → GotUpLate x

-- From "John knows it is not the time to get ready" (we use the content: ¬TimeToGetReady),
-- we can derive that John did NOT go to bed late.
theorem john_not_bedlate_of_not_time : (¬ TimeToGetReady) → ¬ WentToBedLate John := by
  intro hNotTime
  -- From ¬Time and (Alarm → Time), deduce ¬Alarm
  have hNotAlarm : ¬ Alarm := by
    intro hA
    exact hNotTime (R_alarm_time hA)
  -- From Alarm ∨ ¬Late John and ¬Alarm, deduce ¬Late John
  have hNotLateJohn : ¬ Late John := by
    have h := R_alarm_or_notlate John
    cases h with
    | inl hA =>
        exact False.elim (hNotAlarm hA)
    | inr hNL => exact hNL
  -- Now show ¬WentToBedLate John by contradiction chain BedLate → UpLate → MissBus → Late
  intro hBedLate
  have hUp : GotUpLate John := R_bedlate_uplate John hBedLate
  have hMiss : MissBus John := R_uplate_missbus John hUp
  have hLate : Late John := R_missbus_late John hMiss
  exact hNotLateJohn hLate
</lean>

lean_code:
axiom obj : Type
axiom John : obj

axiom Alarm : Prop
axiom TimeToGetReady : Prop

axiom Late : obj → Prop
axiom MissBus : obj → Prop
axiom GotUpLate : obj → Prop
axiom WentToBedLate : obj → Prop

-- If the alarm went off, then it was time to get ready.
axiom R_alarm_time : Alarm → TimeToGetReady
-- If they missed the bus, then they'd be late for school.
axiom R_missbus_late : ∀ x : obj, MissBus x → Late x
-- Either the alarm went off, or they weren't late for school.
axiom R_alarm_or_notlate : ∀ x : obj, Alarm ∨ ¬ Late x
-- If they got up late, then they miss the bus.
axiom R_uplate_missbus : ∀ x : obj, GotUpLate x → MissBus x
-- If they went to bed late, then they get up late.
axiom R_bedlate_uplate : ∀ x : obj, WentToBedLate x → GotUpLate x

-- From "John knows it is not the time to get ready" (we use the content: ¬TimeToGetReady),
-- we can derive that John did NOT go to bed late.
theorem john_not_bedlate_of_not_time : (¬ TimeToGetReady) → ¬ WentToBedLate John := by
  intro hNotTime
  -- From ¬Time and (Alarm → Time), deduce ¬Alarm
  have hNotAlarm : ¬ Alarm := by
    intro hA
    exact hNotTime (R_alarm_time hA)
  -- From Alarm ∨ ¬Late John and ¬Alarm, deduce ¬Late John
  have hNotLateJohn : ¬ Late John := by
    have h := R_alarm_or_notlate John
    cases h with
    | inl hA =>
        exact False.elim (hNotAlarm hA)
    | inr hNL => exact hNL
  -- Now show ¬WentToBedLate John by contradiction chain BedLate → UpLate → MissBus → Late
  intro hBedLate
  have hUp : GotUpLate John := R_bedlate_uplate John hBedLate
  have hMiss : MissBus John := R_uplate_missbus John hUp
  have hLate : Late John := R_missbus_late John hMiss
  exact hNotLateJohn hLate

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Sam : obj

axiom KitchenHot : Prop
axiom StoveOn : Prop

axiom Hungry : obj → Prop
axiom MakePancakes : obj → Prop
axiom UseStove : obj → Prop
axiom AteDinner : obj → Prop

-- If the stove is on, the kitchen gets hot.
axiom R_on_hot : StoveOn → KitchenHot
-- If someone makes pancakes, they use the stove.
axiom R_pancakes_use : ∀ x : obj, MakePancakes x → UseStove x
-- Either the stove was on, or Sam did not use the stove.
axiom D_on_or_notuse : StoveOn ∨ ¬UseStove Sam
-- If someone is hungry, they make pancakes.
axiom R_hungry_pancakes : ∀ x : obj, Hungry x → MakePancakes x
-- If Sam did not eat dinner last night, he was hungry this morning.
axiom R_noDinner_hungry : ¬AteDinner Sam → Hungry Sam

open classical

theorem sam_ate_of_not_hot : ¬KitchenHot → AteDinner Sam := by
  intro hNotHot
  -- From not hot and (On → Hot), deduce the stove was not on.
  have hNotOn : ¬StoveOn := fun hOn => hNotHot (R_on_hot hOn)
  -- From (On ∨ ¬Use Sam) and ¬On, deduce ¬Use Sam.
  have hNotUse : ¬UseStove Sam := by
    cases D_on_or_notuse with
    | inl hOn => exact False.elim (hNotOn hOn)
    | inr h => exact h
  -- Prove AteDinner Sam by contradiction.
  by_contra hNoDinner
  have hHungry : Hungry Sam := R_noDinner_hungry hNoDinner
  have hPancakes : MakePancakes Sam := R_hungry_pancakes Sam hHungry
  have hUse : UseStove Sam := R_pancakes_use Sam hPancakes
  exact hNotUse hUse
</lean>
